Li2S.dat model data is treated as known

The MgH.data model data is taken as known

Ni_supercell.lmp model data is assumed to be known

Use lammps to simulate the tensile process of a high-entropy alloy
Use the deform command to perform tensile operation on the high-entropy alloy.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the melting process of CuNi alloy
First build the CuNi alloy model; hold at 1550K under the NPT ensemble; heat from 1550K to 2550K under the NPT ensemble; cool from 2550K to 2550K under the NPT ensemble

Use lammps to simulate the melting process of Ni
Ni.data model data is taken as known. Simulation method:
(1) Energy minimization
(2) Relaxation

Use LAMMPS to simulate the melting and solidification process of NiZr alloy
NiZr.dat model data is taken as known. Solid solution at 2000K under the NPT ensemble, and cool from 2000K to 300K under the NPT ensemble.

Use LAMMPS to output the RDF (radial distribution function) during the heating process of CuNi alloy melting from 1550K to 2500K.
The melting.in.dat model data is considered known.

Use lammps to output the MSD (mean square displacement) during the heating process of CuNi alloy melting from 1550K-2500K.
The melting.in.dat model data is taken as known.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the smelting process of high-entropy alloys
The cenghe.data model data is taken as known. Simulation method:
(1) Energy minimization
(2) Relaxation
(3) Heating and melting
(4) Heat preservation
(5) Cooling
(6) Relaxation

Simulate the deposition of atoms on a heated atomic substrate.

The Ice.dat model file is assumed to be known.
Use LAMMPS to simulate the crumpling process of a graphene sheet
Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of diamond impacting graphene
Use LAMMPS to simulate and calculate stacking fault energy
Use lammps to simulate a 3D silicon crack described by the SW potential

Use LAMMPS to simulate and calculate the Young's modulus of polycrystalline Ni

Use LAMMPS to simulate and calculate the phonon spectrum of a 3D copper block

Use LAMMPS to simulate and calculate the axial phonons of a 20 nm long (40,40) carbon nanotube.
The data model file is treated as known.
Provide a simple example of a LAMMPS simulation to calculate cohesive energy and lattice constant
Use LAMMPS to simulate and calculate the change of depth and the force on the indenter in the z direction during the nanoindentation process of Cu
Use LAMMPS to perform XRD analysis on the TiNi atomic model before and after phase transition

Calculate thermal conductivity in LAMMPS using the non-equilibrium simulation (NEMD) method
The principle of calculating thermal conductivity under non-equilibrium conditions is to set a heat source and a cold source at the two ends or in the middle of the material. Heat is input at the heat source and an equal amount of heat is extracted at the cold source. During this process, heat transfer occurs inside the material, generating a temperature gradient. In LAMMPS simulations, the energy flux and temperature gradient are obtained, and substituting them into the formula gives the thermal conductivity.

Use the equilibrium molecular dynamics (EMD) method in LAMMPS to calculate the thermal conductivity of graphene.
The gp.data graphene model file is assumed to be known. Calculate the thermal conductivity under equilibrium conditions, with the main formula being Green-Kubo. The main command used is compute heat/flux.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the uniaxial tensile process of graphene using the velocity method.
The velocity method usually fixes one end of the graphene and gives the other end a constant velocity v, and the atoms at this end drive the remaining atoms to move.

Use the deform method in LAMMPS to simulate the process of uniaxial stretching of graphene.
Deform stretches the box at a certain rate. During this process, the atoms of graphene change their coordinates as the box elongates, thereby achieving overall stretching.

Use LAMMPS to simulate crack propagation of SiC under tension
Delete a portion of atoms at the middle position of the initial SiC model to simulate an initial crack.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the stretching process of polyethylene
The pp.data polyethylene model file is assumed to be known.

Use LAMMPS to simulate polymer bond-breaking stretching
The pe.data model file is assumed to be known. To simulate bond breaking during the stretching process, an additional command needs to be added: fix bond/break. This command can be set to check the bond length every N steps; if the bond length exceeds the specified limit length, the bond is considered broken. If the bond-breaking condition is satisfied, the bonding information of this bond is deleted from the entire system.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the shear process of polyethylene PE
The PE.data model file is assumed known. Apply a velocity in a certain direction to the top end of the model to realize the shear motion of the material.

Use lammps to simulate dislocation slip during the shear process of Ni
The Ni.lmp model data is considered known. The simulation process is as follows:
(1) Modeling: according to the method introduced in the lammps tutorial: detailed explanation of the Atomsk modeling method for edge dislocation lines, generate an edge dislocation in Ni, and save the model data file as Ni.lmp.
(2) Read the data file and set upper and lower fixed boundaries.
(3) After generating the dislocation with Atomsk, the system energy is relatively high, and energy minimization is required.
(4) After energy minimization, perform temperature initialization
(5) Use the velocity command to perform shear
Use LAMMPS to simulate the triaxial tensile process of single-crystal Fe
Use LAMMPS to implement nanoindentation simulation of Ni_Graphene composite material

Use LAMMPS to calculate the surface energy of Cu(111)
First calculate the total potential energy E0 of the crystal at equilibrium, then cut the crystal from the middle to form two surfaces, move the crystal apart by a sufficiently large distance, and calculate the total potential energy E1 of the system at this time. Then the surface energy of the crystal is E=(E1-E0)/2A, where A is the surface area.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the lattice constant of Fe
Set a group of lattice constants and use the jump command to run different lattice constants in sequence. Each run calculates one energy value. After the runs are completed, plot the lattice constant–energy curve, and the lattice constant corresponding to the lowest energy value is the lattice constant of Fe.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the formation energy of a Cu interstitial atom
Taking Cu as an example, the simulation process of interstitial atom formation energy is:
(1) Generate Cu containing N atoms, perform energy minimization, and calculate the energy E0.
(2) Randomly insert a C atom at a non-lattice site position.
(3) Perform energy minimization and calculate the energy Ef.
(4) Substitute into the formula to calculate the interstitial atom formation energy Ei.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the vacancy formation energy of Cu
Assume the system has a total of N atoms. After deleting one atom, the total number of atoms becomes (N-1). The vacancy formation energy equals the energy Ef after deleting one atom minus the energy Ei of (N-1) atoms.

Use the reactive potential reax in LAMMPS to simulate the hydrogen combustion process. The H2_O2.data model file is assumed to be known. Output the reactant types and the number of each reactant every 100 steps, save the data to the species.out file, and it can be programmatically used to count the variation of each reactant with time.

Use the ReaxFF potential in LAMMPS to simulate the high-temperature reaction process of Fe(OH)3
The FeOH.data model file is treated as known.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the stacking fault energy of Ni
The stacking fault energy can be calculated through the energy difference before and after the formation of the stacking fault. The calculation formula is: (Ef-E0)/A. E0 is the initial energy, Ef is the energy after deleting one atomic plane, and A is the interface area.
Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of fluid flowing around obstacles
Use lammps to simulate the performance of the CHO system under the reactive force field reax/c

Use LAMMPS to simulate the CNT tensile process
data.mysystem model file is assumed to be known. Repeatedly call the displace_atoms command to gradually move the fixed ends to both sides to achieve stretching.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of molecular free-fall motion
Randomly generate a certain number of molecules above the box, which reach the bottom of the box after free fall. Set a "wall" at the bottom of the box to prevent atoms from running out of the box. The molecules are set as rigid bodies and stop moving after colliding with the bottom wall.

Use LAMMPS to simulate a crosslinking reaction
the data.lammps model file is taken as known. The middle atoms move randomly; when they satisfy the bonding condition with the atoms on the outer wall of the cylinder, a crosslinking reaction occurs, generating new bond connections.

Use lammps to simulate the relaxation process of graphene oxide and water molecules
data.lammps model file is assumed known.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the Al impact process
In LAMMPS impact simulations, the impact process generally uses the NVE ensemble. The simulation of impact is relatively simple; after relaxation is completed, set the substrate to remain stationary, and set the impacting object with a relatively high velocity to rush toward the substrate.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the compression process of single-crystal copper
The compression process is the reverse tensile process.

Use lammps to simulate the interface between Al and polyurethane
The pu_al_data.data model file is taken as known. When metal and polymer are mixed into one system, unit conversion is required.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the Ni shearing process
The principle of shearing is to set upper and lower boundaries; during shearing the lower boundary is fixed, and a moving velocity is set for the upper boundary.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the atomic diffusion process at the Cu and Al interface
The principle of simulating diffusion with LAMMPS is relatively simple; set a relatively long relaxation time at a certain temperature.

Use lammps to set the CO2 small molecule as a rigid body
The co2.data model file is assumed to be known.

Using LAMMPS graphene adsorbing water molecules process (gcmc)
The channel material of the upper and lower walls is graphene, and the adsorbed molecules are TIP4P water molecules.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the interface energy of Cu
Under periodic boundary ppp, calculate the potential energy of the system Ebulk, then modify the boundary condition to mpp and calculate the new potential energy Ecut. After the boundary is changed to m boundary, the atoms on the left and right sides in the x direction no longer interact with each other, which also achieves the effect of cutting out two planes. Substituting into the formula gives the interface energy: (Ecut-Ebulk)/s. Where s is the interface area.

Use lammps to calculate the cohesive energy of Fe atoms (Cohesive Energy)
Calculate the total potential energy of the system, then divide by the number of atoms.

Use LAMMPS to simulate and calculate the melting point of a crystalline material
Use the NPT ensemble to control heating, output temperature and potential energy during the melting process, plot the temperature–potential energy curve, and the temperature at which the potential energy changes abruptly is the melting point.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the evaporation process of a water sphere
The H2O.data model file is assumed to be known. The initial temperature is 273K, heat to 473K under the NPT ensemble, then keep 473K under the NVT ensemble to realize evaporation.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the evaporation process of Ar on a Cu substrate
The substrate is Cu, and the atoms above are Ar atoms. The initial temperature is 90 K. The temperature of the Cu substrate is increased from 90 K to 160 K and then maintained at 160 K. During this process, Ar gains energy and evaporates.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the CO2 diffusion process
During the diffusion process calculate and output the MSD, and the diffusion coefficient can be calculated from the MSD.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the heating and melting process of NaCl
The nacl.data model file is assumed to be known. The potential function uses born/coul/long, and a loop is used to simulate multiple temperatures in the same in file, performing npt relaxation at different temperatures.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of water molecules flowing inside a graphene slit
data.lammps model file is assumed to be known. Key points: (1) Water molecules are TIP4P-type water molecules. In the simulation, the fix addforce command is used to drive the water molecules to generate flow.
(2) To prevent graphene from moving, use the fix command to keep the graphene position relatively fixed:
(3) In the temperature calculation of water molecules, the influence of the velocity in the flow direction on temperature is removed

Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of a membrane permeation filtration simulation
The entire simulation system contains a total of 5 types of atoms, with the corresponding types as follows: type 1: left piston wall; type 2: middle membrane; type 3: right piston wall; type 4: solvent atoms; type 5: solute atoms

Use LAMMPS to simulate the equilibrium relaxation process of membrane permeation filtration
data.lammps model file is regarded as known.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the influence of different porosities on filtration performance in membrane permeation filtration
data.lammps model file is taken as known. Deleting a certain number of atoms can form pores or vacancy defects.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the pouring process. Add a certain number of atoms into the box at regular time intervals, and the number of atoms in the box increases with the simulation time. The command used is fix pour, which can be used to simulate the effect of pouring particles into a container under the action of gravity.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of water molecules being adsorbed inside a CNT (gcmc)
Files such as data.lammps, PARM.lammps, and TIP4P2005.lammps are treated as known.

Use lammps to simulate the nucleation process of water molecules in a gas environment inside a graphene slit
the graphite.data model file is regarded as known. In the initial model, water molecules and CO2 molecules are fully mixed into the graphene channel. Under the nvt, nve and npt ensembles respectively, water molecules aggregate to the central position, while CO2 disperses into upper and lower two layers.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of laser heating a Cu sphere
The cu.data model file is assumed to be known. The principle of simulating a laser in LAMMPS is to input the laser energy into the irradiated region, and the command used is fix heat; the laser energy is high and the duration is short.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the shot peening process
There are many methods to simulate the shot peening process with LAMMPS, such as the velocity impact method, the fix deposit deposition method, etc. The principles are basically similar: give the sphere a specific velocity to impact the substrate. The velocity impact method is used; a diamond small sphere impacts the Si substrate at high speed, leaving an impact pit on the Si surface after the collision.

Use the NEMD algorithm on LAMMPS to simulate the thermal conductivity of water molecules
Set a heat source and a cold source in the heat transfer direction, continuously input heat at the heat source, and continuously remove heat at the cold source, thereby generating a temperature gradient. After the simulation is completed, substitute parameters such as heat flux and temperature gradient into the formula to calculate the thermal conductivity.
Simulate fluid using lammps

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of molecular deposition
The principle of molecular deposition is basically the same as atomic deposition, treating molecules as atoms.

Use lammps to simulate the sintering process of Ag spheres
After modeling is completed, use nvt or npt ensemble to control heating to complete the sintering process.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the angular distribution (ADF) of O-O-O and H-O-H in a water molecular system
data.spce model file is assumed to be known. Similar to the radial distribution rdf, LAMMPS provides a command to compute angular distributions: compute adf. This command can collect the distribution data of a certain type of angle in the system (such as the H-O-H angle of water molecules).

Use LAMMPS to simulate the compression process of hydrogen
The H.data model file is assumed to be known. Simulation temperature 300k, pressure 100GPa, under this condition hydrogen should be solid.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of random collisions of small balls
Randomly generate 800 red balls (type 1) and 1 blue ball (type 2). After obtaining initial velocities, the balls collide with each other.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the hindered movement process of a polymer chain
The poly.input model file is regarded as known. The polymer chain data is in the model file and is read in by the read_data command. Blue small spheres are randomly generated, and the number can be set arbitrarily. During the simulation process, the polymer chain can move, while the blue small spheres remain fixed. When the number of small spheres is small, the movement of the polymer chain is not affected; when the number of blue spheres is large, the movement of the polymer chain is hindered.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the gas diffusion phenomenon
Randomly fill 100 gas atoms in three-dimensional space. After initializing the temperature, the atoms obtain initial velocities. Under the NVE ensemble, the potential energy and kinetic energy of the atoms convert into each other under the LJ force field, producing a diffusion phenomenon. The higher the temperature, the faster the diffusion speed.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the RDF of a two-dimensional liquid equilibrium state. Randomly generate 400*0.8 atoms and perform temperature initialization, the atoms move under the LJ force field to certain positions and then reach equilibrium, and output rdf data.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the melting process of a gold sphere
After setting the lattice, build a 15*15*15 lattice box, and fill the box entirely with Au atoms. Set a spherical region (region) in the box, use the delete_atoms command to delete Au atoms outside the spherical region, to realize the modeling of a gold sphere. The temperature is increased from 298K to 2400K.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the cluster melting process.
The simulation units are LJ, a 2D simulation system, periodic boundary conditions, and the force field is the most common LJ force field. Simulation idea:
(1) Set up a 40*40 simulation box, create a 20*20 region at the center of the box, and randomly fill atoms in the central region.
(2) Perform energy minimization to eliminate overlap of randomly generated atoms.
(3) Set the LJ force field.
(4) Set the NVE ensemble and use Langevin temperature control for temperature regulation. This step can also be directly replaced by the NVT ensemble.
(5) Run and output the trajectory file.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the deposition process of C atoms on a Si substrate
The substrate is Si, and C atoms are deposited onto the Si surface at a certain velocity.

Use the addforce method in lammps to implement stretching of the chain
The poly.txt model file is given. The simulation uses LJ units. The initial structure is a chain composed of 40 atoms, with bonds set between atoms, totaling 39 bonds. When setting the force field, the pair potential is ignored and only the bond interaction force is calculated. The bond potential function is the fene potential. During the simulation, a time-varying force is applied to the rightmost atom.

Use lammps to analyze the generation process of clusters during the melting of Cu
Within a group, when the distance between atoms is less than a certain distance, this group of atoms can be considered a cluster. Use the lammps cluster calculation command compute ID group-ID cluster/atom cutoff, where cutoff is the cutoff radius.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of a sphere rotating around a fixed axis
Use the fix move rotate command.

LAMMPS sets the simulation parameters for the NaCl core-shell model
In the model, one atom is divided into two parts: core and shell. During modeling, the core and shell of one atom are built as two separate atom models. The core and shell atoms can be located at the same position, or they can have a slight deviation, and there is a bond connecting the core and the shell.

Use lammps to simulate the torsion process
There are two ways of torsion: one is fixing one end and rotating the other end, the second is rotating both ends in opposite directions. The command for rotation is the fix move rotate command. The two-side rotation method is adopted.

Use lammps to simulate the stretching process of polyethylene glycol (PEG) in an aqueous solution environment.
The mix.data model file is considered known. There are usually two methods for stretching in lammps simulations: fix deform stretching and velocity stretching. These two methods are mostly used for the overall deformation of the simulation box. In this simulation, PEG is located in the middle of the box, surrounded by an aqueous solution environment. During the PEG stretching process, the size of the entire box remains relatively stable. Therefore, the fix addforce command is used to apply forces in opposite directions to the atoms at both ends of the PEG chain, achieving the effect of stretching PEG in water.

Use lammps to calculate the binding energy of SiC
The sic.data model file is taken as known. The energy released when atoms combine into a stable crystal is called the binding energy of the crystal (binding energy), also called the cohesive energy (cohesive energy). Calculate the total potential energy of the system, then divide by the number of atoms.

Use LAMMPS to simulate the process of shear flow of a solution. The system.data model file is assumed to be known. Set the force on wall atoms in the x direction to 0, then use the velocity command to set the upper and lower walls to move in opposite directions to generate shear flow. First divide the system into blocks along a certain direction, then count the velocity of atoms within each block. After combining the outputs, the velocity distribution along a certain direction can be obtained.

Use LAMMPS to dynamically add atoms into the system
Use the fix pour command to simulate inserting 3000 atoms into the system, and the process in which the atoms deposit to the bottom under gravity.

Use LAMMPS to dynamically delete atoms in the system
Use the fix evaporate command, taking gas evaporation diffusion as an example. Set a top region inside the box; when atoms enter the top region, delete 10 atoms every 100 steps.

Use LAMMPS to implement Monte Carlo (MC) modeling of FeCu alloy
The MC run interval is 100 steps, and the final concentration ratio is 0.02, that is, 2% of Fe atoms are exchanged to Cu atoms.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the volumetric heat capacity of Cu
Under the condition of keeping the system volume unchanged, calculate the corresponding total energy of the system at different temperatures

Use LAMMPS to calculate the bulk modulus of diamond
Need to obtain the relationship between single-atom energy and lattice constant

Use LAMMPS to calculate the equilibrium lattice constant of a diamond crystal
It is required to obtain the relationship between single-atom energy and lattice constant

Use LAMMPS to calculate the equilibrium lattice constant of Al
It is required to obtain the relationship between the single-atom energy and the lattice constant. Use the conjugate gradient (cg) method to minimize the total energy of the system to find the stable state of the system at each lattice constant, and output different lattice constants and the corresponding average potential energy.

Use LAMMPS to calculate the binding energy of diamond crystal
Need to obtain the relationship between single-atom energy and lattice constant

Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of the high-entropy alloy AlCuFeMgSi
Main process:

(1) Determine the general lattice structure, whether it is fcc or bcc; these can be obtained by consulting the literature.

(2) Determine the lattice constant; using the largest lattice constant among the alloy elements is sufficient, though it is not necessary to choose the largest lattice constant, and other values can also be selected. The lattice constant is only used for initial modeling; after relaxation an accurate lattice constant value will be obtained.

(3) Generate the box, and fill the box with atoms of type 1.

(4) The set type/ratio command randomly selects atoms of type 1 according to a ratio and converts them into other types of atoms.

Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a series of regularly arranged nano copper spheres, achieving an array feature structure
Using the replicate command can create a supercell, and can also create structures with array features.
Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a cylindrical Ni and graphene layer composite material, inserting one layer of graphene into the cylindrical Ni nanopillar

Use lammps to implement the modeling of a NiTi alloy
Modeling process:
(1) Use the lattice custom command to generate a square box, set the basis parameters, and determine the atomic positions. Vertex atom coordinates (0,0,0), center atom coordinates (0.5,0.5,0.5). (2) Use create_atoms and basis to specify the vertex atom type as 2 and the center atom type as 1. (3) Use mass to define 1 as Ni atoms and 2 as Ti atoms.
Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of Cu twin crystal

Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of NiTi alloy and divide three working regions
Method: (1) divide three regions

(2) respectively use the create_atoms command within these three regions to fill different types of atoms
Use lammps to implement the modeling of an Al spherical shell model, the inner core is an Al sphere, and the outside is wrapped with a layer of Al spherical shell
Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a water sphere model
Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of desalinating seawater with graphene
Use lammps to implement modeling of Al atom segregation at a Cu grain boundary
Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a tilted conical tool

Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a filleted tool
Method:
(1) Create the fillet region
(2) Create the upper rectangular region
(3) Create the lower rectangular region
(4) Merge the 3 regions together
Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a triangular tool

Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of amorphous SiO2
The sio2.data model file is assumed to be known.

Use lammps to implement modeling of a CO2 molecular model
Create a box and randomly add 400 CO2 molecules
Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of a 1D sinusoidal rough interface
Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of a 2D sinusoidal rough interface
Use lammps to perform modeling, with Si on the upper part and Cu on the lower part, a Si/Cu mixed region at the interface, and reserve a crack in the mixed region.

Use lammps to implement the modeling of hcp-type Mg
The official version defaults a parameter, and the lattice constant needs to be customized.

Use lammps to rotate GaN 90 degrees along the x-axis
GaN.data model file is given

Use lammps to implement modeling of a rough interface
Build the smallest periodic unit cell and use the array command to replicate.
Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of a fluid model, with the upper and lower walls composed of Al atoms, and the fluid being a NaCl solution (including water molecules, Na+ and Cl-)
Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a semi-ellipsoidal indentation model
Use lammps to model a graphene sheet and rotate it, the rotation angle is 45 degrees
Use LAMMPS to model a copper crystal block

Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of randomly inserting graphene sheets into a Cu matrix
cu.data and gp.data two model files are treated as known
Use LAMMPS to add 60 TIP5P water molecules into the system
Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of amorphous Cu

Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of adding a crystalline Cu sphere into amorphous Cu
The cu1.data model file is regarded as known
Use LAMMPS for modeling; in the middle of the model is a water sphere, and outside the water sphere is CO2 gas

Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of an acute-angle cutting tool
Set three regions, use the intersect command to merge them into a closed region, and then generate atoms within this region

Use LAMMPS to implement the modeling of a diamond triangular pyramid tool
Combine three inclined planes or three inclined planes plus one plane into a closed region, and generate atoms in the region.

Use LAMMPS to implement modeling of a hexagonal structure
Use region plane to set 6 faces, use these 6 faces to form a closed region, and generate atoms inside the closed region.

Use LAMMPS to implement hexahedral core–shell structure modeling
The inner core is Cu atoms, and the outer layer is coated with a certain thickness of Cu atoms. Create inner-core and outer-shell regions, and use the create_atoms command to fill atoms in the two regions.

Use LAMMPS to implement spherical shell structure modeling
The core is Al atoms, and the outer layer is coated with Al atoms of a certain thickness. Method:
(1) Create two spherical regions, inner and outer spheres
(2) Fill type 1 atoms in the outer sphere region
(3) Delete the type 1 atoms filled in the inner sphere region
(4) Fill type 2 atoms in the inner sphere region

Use LAMMPS to implement capsule particle molecular model modeling
Take Cu atoms as an example. Create three regions: upper hemisphere region, cylindrical region, lower hemisphere region

Use lammps to implement nanotube model construction
Create three regions: inner cylindrical region, outer cylindrical region, tube region
Use lammps to implement high-entropy alloy modeling
Use lammps to implement composite material/laminate structure modeling

Use lammps to implement capsule core-shell structure modeling
It is necessary to create the following regions twice: upper hemisphere region, cylindrical region, lower hemisphere region
